texto: Kahneman’s evidence shows that we suck at remembering and predicting our own well-being. We as a culture still ignore this empirical evidence, recommending to live our lives so as to avoid deathbed regrets. Deathbed regrets are like Hollywood films: they stir passions for a couple hours, but are poorly connected to reality. They are not good criteria for a well-lived life.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Nick Winter: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “Kahneman’s evidence shows that we suck at remembering and predicting our own well-being. We as a …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que felicidade, happiness, regret pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de luto e termino.
